title: AA Similarity Criterion
description: Two triangles are similar if they have two pairs of congruent angles.
condition: Given two triangles with two corresponding angles equal.
response: Conclude the triangles are similar.
label: Principle / Model
example: If two triangles each have angles 90° and 40°, then they are similar by AA.

title: Apply Dilation from the Origin
description: Given a point and a scale factor with center at (0,0), compute the coordinates of its image by multiplying both coordinates by the scale factor.
condition: A point (x, y) is dilated from center (0,0) with scale factor k.
response: Compute image as (kx, ky).
label: Principle / Model
example: Dilate (3, -4) by scale factor 2 from (0,0): $(2\cdot3, 2\cdot(-4)) = (6, -8)$.
notes: Core coordinate rule for dilations centered at the origin.

title: Apply Scale Factor to Find Corresponding Side
description: Multiply a known side length by the scale factor to find the corresponding side in a similar triangle.
condition: Given a scale factor between similar triangles and a known side length in one triangle.
response: Multiply the known side length by the scale factor to compute the corresponding side length.
label: Rule
example: If the scale factor is $\frac{5}{3}$ and a side is $\frac{7}{3}$, then the corresponding side is $\frac{5}{3} \cdot \frac{7}{3} = \frac{35}{9}$.

title: Check if Point Lies on Line
description: Determine whether a point lies on a line by substituting into the line’s equation.
condition: Given a linear equation and a coordinate point.
response: Substitute the coordinates; if the equation is true, conclude the point lies on the line.
label: Principle / Model
example: For $y = 3x - 2$, test $(4,10)$: $10 = 3(4) - 2 = 10$, so the point lies on the line.

title: Compute Scale Factor from Corresponding Sides
description: Determine the scale factor by forming a ratio of corresponding side lengths in similar triangles.
condition: Given two corresponding side lengths from similar triangles.
response: Divide one corresponding side length by the other to determine the scale factor.
label: Rule
example: If corresponding sides are $5$ and $3$, then the scale factor (large to small) is $\frac{5}{3}$.

title: Compute Slope from Two Points
description: Calculate the slope of a line using two points by finding the ratio of vertical change to horizontal change.
condition: Given two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) on a line.
response: Compute slope as (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1).
label: Principle / Model

title: Constant Scale Factor for Similarity
description: For two polygons to be similar, all corresponding side lengths must be related by the same scale factor.
condition: Given two polygons with measured corresponding sides.
response: Check whether ratios of all corresponding sides are equal; if not, conclude they are not similar.
label: Principle / Model
example: If one pair of sides has ratio $2$ but another pair has ratio $1.5$, then the polygons are not similar.

title: Corresponding Sides of Similar Triangles Are Proportional
description: Use the property that corresponding sides in similar triangles have equal ratios.
condition: Given two triangles known to be similar.
response: Set up equal ratios of corresponding side lengths.
label: Principle
example: If $\triangle ABC \sim \triangle DEF$, then $\frac{AB}{DE} = \frac{BC}{EF} = \frac{AC}{DF}$.

title: Dilated Point Lies on Ray from Center
description: Under a dilation, the image of a point lies on the line through the center and the original point.
condition: Given a dilation with center P and original point A.
response: Place the image of A on line PA at a distance scaled by the scale factor.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If the scale factor is 2 and $PA = 3$, then the image $A'$ lies on ray $PA$ with $PA' = 6$.

title: Dilations Preserve Angle Measure
description: A dilation keeps all corresponding angle measures equal.
condition: Given a polygon and its image under dilation.
response: Conclude corresponding angles are congruent.
label: Concept
example: If triangle $ABC$ is dilated to $A'B'C'$, then $\angle A = \angle A'$.

title: Dilations Produce Similar Figures
description: A dilation of a figure results in a figure similar to the original.
condition: A figure is transformed by a dilation with any nonzero scale factor.
response: Conclude the image is similar to the original figure.
label: Concept
example: If triangle $ABC$ is dilated by scale factor $\tfrac{1}{2}$, then $\triangle ABC \sim \triangle A'B'C'$.

title: Identify Center of Dilation by Collinearity
description: Determine the center of dilation by finding the point through which lines connecting corresponding vertices of a figure and its image pass.
condition: Given a preimage and image under dilation on a plane.
response: Locate the common intersection point of lines drawn through each pair of corresponding vertices; this point is the center of dilation.
label: Principle / Model
example: If lines connecting corresponding vertices of triangles $L$ and $M$ all pass through point $C$, then $C$ is the center of dilation.
notes: Relies on geometric property that dilation maps points along rays from the center.

title: Identify Corresponding Vertices in Similar Triangles
description: Match vertices of two triangles based on naming order or geometric position to determine corresponding sides.
condition: Given a statement that triangle ABC is similar to triangle DEF.
response: Match A↔D, B↔E, C↔F to identify corresponding sides and angles.
label: Concept
example: If $\triangle ABC \sim \triangle DEF$, then $AB \leftrightarrow DE$, $BC \leftrightarrow EF$, and $AC \leftrightarrow DF$.

title: Interpret Slope as Rise over Run
description: Understand slope as the quotient of vertical change divided by horizontal change.
condition: Given a line or slope triangle on a coordinate plane.
response: Express slope as vertical change / horizontal change.
label: Concept
example: If a line rises 2 units and runs 4 units, slope = 2/4 = 1/2.

title: Match Line to Given Slope on Graph
description: Identify which graphed line corresponds to a specified slope by comparing steepness (rise over run).
condition: Given multiple lines on a coordinate grid and specified slope values.
response: Select or label the line whose rise/run matches each given slope.
label: Production / Skill
example: A line that rises 1 unit for every 1 unit right has slope 1; the steepest line rising 3 units for every 1 unit right has slope 3.
notes: Visual comparison of steepness; often intuitive.

title: Recognize Effect of Scale Factor on Distance
description: Understand that a dilation multiplies all distances from the center by the scale factor.
condition: A dilation with scale factor k and center C is applied to a figure.
response: Conclude that each image point lies on the ray from C through the original point at k times the original distance.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $CP = 3$ units and the scale factor is 2, then $CP' = 6$ units along the same ray.
notes: Supports both graphical identification of center and coordinate computation.

title: Recognize Equivalent Point-Slope Equations
description: Determine whether different point-slope equations describe the same line by checking slope and reference point consistency.
condition: Given multiple equations of the form (y - y1)/(x - x1) = m.
response: Identify equations that use correct slope and a point on the line.
label: Principle / Model
example: Using point (7,6) and slope 1/2: (y - 6)/(x - 7) = 1/2 describes the same line as (y - 4)/(x - 3) = 1/2.

title: Solve Proportion by Cross Multiplication
description: Solve an equation of the form $\frac{a}{b} = \frac{c}{x}$ by cross multiplying and isolating the unknown.
condition: Given a proportion involving a single unknown variable.
response: Cross multiply and solve the resulting equation for the unknown value.
label: Principle
example: If $\frac{3}{5} = \frac{\frac{7}{3}}{x}$, then $3x = 5 \cdot \frac{7}{3}$, so $x = \frac{35}{9}$.

title: Triangle Angle Sum
description: The interior angles of a triangle sum to 180 degrees.
condition: Given two interior angles of a triangle and needing the third angle.
response: Compute the third angle as 180° minus the sum of the two given angles.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a triangle has angles 90° and 40°, then the third angle is $180 - (90 + 40) = 50°$.

title: Write Equation Using Point-Slope Form
description: Given a point and slope, write an equation of the line using point-slope form.
condition: Given slope m and point $(x_1, y_1)$ on a line.
response: Write equation $y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)$.
label: Principle / Model
example: With slope $3$ through $(2,4)$: $y - 4 = 3(x - 2)$.
